title: Run Error Handling and Recovery
activity_flow: START: Agent turn loop increments current_turn counter
DECISION: current_turn > max_turns?
[YES] → Attach SpanError('Max turns exceeded') to current tracing span
→ Instantiate MaxTurnsExceeded exception (not raised yet)
→ Call build_run_error_data(input, new_items, raw_responses, last_agent, reasoning_item_id_policy)
→ Converts input to history list via ItemHelpers.input_to_new_input_list()
→ Converts each RunItem to TResponseInputItem via run_item_to_input_item()
→ Returns RunErrorData(input, new_items, history, output, raw_responses, last_agent)
→ Call resolve_run_error_handler_result(error_handlers, error, context_wrapper, run_data)
DECISION: error_handlers is None or empty?
[YES] → return None
DECISION: error_handlers.get('max_turns') is None?
[YES] → return None
→ Build RunErrorHandlerInput(error=MaxTurnsExceeded, context=RunContextWrapper, run_data=RunErrorData)
→ Invoke user handler: result = handler(handler_input)  [sync or async]
→ DECISION: inspect.isawaitable(result)?
[YES] → await result
→ DECISION: result is None? → return None
→ DECISION: result is RunErrorHandlerResult? → return result as-is
→ DECISION: result is dict with 'final_output' key?
[YES, valid keys] → return RunErrorHandlerResult(**result)
[YES, extra keys] → raise UserError('Invalid run error handler result.')
→ DECISION: result is any other value? → wrap: return RunErrorHandlerResult(final_output=result)
BACK IN run_loop: DECISION: handler_result is None?
[YES] → raise MaxTurnsExceeded (no recovery, propagates as unhandled error)
[NO] → Continue with recovery path:
→ Call validate_handler_final_output(current_agent, handler_result.final_output)
→ Get output_schema via get_output_schema(agent)
→ DECISION: schema is None or plain text? → return final_output unchanged
→ Serialize to JSON, validate via output_schema.validate_json()
→ DECISION: serialization/validation error? → raise UserError('Invalid run error handler final_output for structured output.')
→ Call format_final_output_text(current_agent, validated_output) → str
→ Call create_message_output_item(current_agent, output_text) → MessageOutputItem
→ Constructs ResponseOutputMessage(role='assistant', status='completed')
→ DECISION: handler_result.include_in_history == True?
[YES] → Append synthesized MessageOutputItem to generated_items and session_items
→ Persist to session if session persistence enabled
→ Run final output hooks: run_final_output_hooks(agent, hooks, context_wrapper, validated_output)
→ Run output guardrails: run_output_guardrails(..., validated_output, ...)
→ Construct RunResult(input, new_items, raw_responses, final_output=validated_output, ...)
→ Set result._current_turn = max_turns
→ Return RunResult (recovery complete)
[NO from 'current_turn > max_turns'] → Continue normal turn processing
PARALLEL PATH — BaseException catch block (any error during run loop):
→ Catch BaseException as exc
→ DECISION: isinstance(exc, AgentsException)?
[YES] → Attach RunErrorDetails(input, new_items, raw_responses, last_agent, context_wrapper, guardrail_results) to exc.run_data
→ re-raise exc (no recovery, just enriches exception with run data)
STREAMING PATH (run_loop streaming variant):
→ Same build_run_error_data + resolve_run_error_handler_result pattern
→ DECISION: handler_result is None?
[YES, handler_configured=True] → streamed_result._max_turns_handled = False → put QueueCompleteSentinel → break
[YES, handler_configured=False] → put QueueCompleteSentinel → break (MaxTurnsExceeded propagates via queue)
[NO] → validate, format, synthesize item, conditionally append to streamed_result items
END

key_data_structures.RunErrorData: input, new_items, history, output, raw_responses, last_agent
key_data_structures.RunErrorHandlerInput: error: MaxTurnsExceeded, context: RunContextWrapper, run_data: RunErrorData
key_data_structures.RunErrorHandlers: TypedDict with optional key 'max_turns' → RunErrorHandler callable

key_decisions: max_turns exceeded? → trigger error handling path
error_handlers registered? → attempt recovery vs. raise
handler returns RunErrorHandlerResult / dict / raw value → normalize to RunErrorHandlerResult
include_in_history flag → controls whether synthesized output enters conversation history
output schema structured? → validate and serialize handler output accordingly
AgentsException subclass? → enrich with RunErrorDetails before re-raise

error_types.MaxTurnsExceeded: Only currently handled error kind; triggers 'max_turns' handler lookup
error_types.AgentsException: Any SDK exception; enriched with run_data (RunErrorDetails) before re-raise
error_types.UserError: Raised when handler returns invalid result or incompatible final_output type
error_types.ModelBehaviorError: Raised internally when structured output validation fails; re-wrapped as UserError

important_notes: The error handler mechanism is opt-in via error_handlers={'max_turns': fn} parameter on Runner.run() / Runner.run_sync() / Runner.stream(). Only MaxTurnsExceeded is recoverable via handler; all other exceptions propagate after being enriched with run context data. Handlers can be sync or async. The synthesized MessageOutputItem uses FAKE_RESPONSES_ID as a placeholder response ID. include_in_history=False allows returning a final_output without polluting the conversation context.
